rulings.Case Title: MIGUEL DEJEAN VS. ASM GLOBAL AT MOSCONE CENTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Proceeding; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, February 26, 2026, Line 1. RESPONDENT ASM GLOBAL's Motion To Dismiss Proceeding. Defendant ASM's Motion is GRANTED. Moving party to submit a proposed order for the Court's review. If the tentative is contested, the matter will be heard on March 12, 2026 at 1:00 p.m before Judge True in department 302. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMT)

rulings.Case Title: RODNEY HAMPTON, JR. VS. LUKE O’BRIEN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, February 26, 2026, Line 5. 1 - PLAINTIFFs RODNEY HAMPTON, JR., AND DERIVATIVELY ON BEHALF OF CDXX, LLC.'s Motion To Set Aside Permit. Plaintiff's motion to set aside permit is DENIED. Plaintiff's motion does not cogently explain how the court has the authority to provide the requested relief. The first amended complaint does not plead a claim regarding the permit amendment and the pleadings determine materiality. (Teselle v. McLoughlin (2009) 173 Cal.app.4th 156, 172 ["Materiality depends on the issues in the case, and what matters are at issue is determined by the pleadings"].) Plaintiff does not establish a basis for the court to set aside the permit amendment, which the City and County of San Francisco accepted. While the first amended complaint references defendants' taxes, defendants' have a privacy interest in their tax documents and plaintiff does not present a reasonable basis to compel disclosure. If the tentative is contested, the matter will be heard on March 12, 2026 at 1:00 p.m before Judge True in department 302. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMT)

rulings.Case Title: RODNEY HAMPTON, JR. VS. LUKE O’BRIEN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Cause Of Action; Declaration; Evidence
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, February 26, 2026, Line 5. 4 - Plaintiff's motion for sanctions is DENIED. Plaintiff does not establish a basis to sanction defense counsel. Plaintiff fails to show that defendant's demurrer was frivolous or compliance with the safe harbor provision of CCP 128.7. It appears that plaintiff served the motion on 1/19/26 and then filed it on 1/22/26. Plaintiff failed to provide the required 21-days safe harbor period. (CCP 128.7(c)(1).) If the tentative is contested, the matter will be heard on March 12, 2026 at 1:00 p.m before Judge True in department 302. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: RODNEY HAMPTON, JR. VS. LUKE O’BRIEN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition And Motion To Vacate Arbitration Award; Reuest For Stay Of Enforcement
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, February 26, 2026, Line 5. 2 - PLAINTIFFs RODNEY HAMPTON, JR., AND DERIVATIVELY ON BEHALF OF CDXX, LLC.'s Motion To Vacate Arbitration Award; Reuest For Stay Of Enforcement. Plaintiff's motion to vacate the arbitration award is DENIED. By order dated 1/15/26, the court denied plaintiff's motion to vacate the arbitration award with prejudice. Plaintiff has not demonstrated a basis for this court to reconsider that order or allow a renewed motion for the same relief. CCP 1008 requires new facts, new law, or new circumstances. In any event, plaintiff does not establish a basis for vacatur under the limited grounds of CCP 1286.2. "Every reasonable intendment is indulged to give effect to arbitration proceedings; the burden is on the party attacking the award to affirmatively establish the existence of error by a proper record." (Lopes v. Millsap (1992) 6 Cal.App.4th 1679, 1685.) If the tentative is contested, the matter will be heard on March 12, 2026 at 1:00 p.m before Judge True in department 302. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: CRISTAL OPONE VS. METWEST TERRA HOSPITALITY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Quash Subpoenas Duces Tecum And For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, February 26, 2026, Line 8. 1 - PLAINTIFF CRISTAL OPONE's Motion To Quash Subpoenas Duces Tecum And For Protective Order. The Court GRANTS Plaintiffs Motions to Quash Defendants' Subpoenas duces tecum without prejudice to their reissuance limiting their scope to records referring to Plaintiff's treatment for skin conditions only for a period of five (5) years prior to the subpoenas' issuance. Plaintiff to prepare a proposed Protective Order to that effect and present it to Defendants for review and to the Court for approval. If the tentative is contested, the matter will be heard on March 12, 2026 at 1:00 p.m before Judge True in department 302.
